# Blueprint

Blueprint turns one real facility, one robot task, and one pass bar into a capture-backed readiness report before an expensive robot pilot. This file is a machine-readable website map, not a permissions file or a live-readiness proof. Crawler access is controlled by `/robots.txt`.

## Core Definitions

- Site/task readiness report: an advisory packet for one real facility, robot task suite, robot profile, threshold set, failure modes, missing proof, and recommended next step.
- Site package: walkthrough media, route context, poses, metadata, geometry when available, rights/privacy posture, provenance, and export scope for one site.
- Hosted evaluation: a managed buyer review room for a site package when package, entitlement, and runtime proof support that request.
- Capture provenance: the capture record, timestamps, device/context metadata, privacy handling, rights posture, freshness, and restrictions attached to downstream outputs.
- Ground-truth boundary: raw capture evidence, timestamps, poses, device metadata, rights/privacy records, provenance, and package or runtime artifacts are stronger than public samples, generated previews, dry-run commerce, catalog matches, or request drafts.
- Public sample: representative material that shows product shape. It is not customer proof, rights clearance, provider execution, payment success, hosted fulfillment, or a deployment verdict.

## High-Priority Public Pages

- [Home](https://tryblueprint.io/): the core buyer story, including value prop, workflow, planning ranges, proof boundary, and request CTA.
- [Pricing](https://tryblueprint.io/pricing): planning ranges for site/task readiness review, hosted evaluation, and custom multi-site benchmark work.
- [Proof](https://tryblueprint.io/proof): concise proof explainer separating public samples from request-specific evidence and owner-system proof.
- [Contact](https://tryblueprint.io/contact): structured intake for robot-team readiness review requests.
- [Privacy](https://tryblueprint.io/privacy): privacy policy.
- [Terms](https://tryblueprint.io/terms): terms of service.

Secondary marketing URLs such as `/product`, `/readiness`, `/for-robot-teams`, `/how-it-works`, `/world-models`, `/agents`, `/capture`, `/sample-deliverables`, `/launch-map`, `/faq`, `/governance`, `/about`, `/updates`, `/careers`, and `/help` are no longer primary public buyer surfaces. They redirect, remain hidden, or serve direct operational/support purposes only.

## Agent Request Pattern

Robot-team agents may construct request URLs without scraping the UI, or use CLI `request location` / MCP `blueprint.request.locationDraft` to receive a dry-run draft packet with `contactUrl`, `inboundRequestDraft`, `missingRequiredFields`, `truthBoundaries`, and `submitInstructions`:

`/contact?source=site-worlds&buyerType=robot_team&path=hosted-review&location=123%20Unknown%20St&workflow=warehouse%20tote&message=Need%20a%20hosted%20review`

Supported request fields include `source`, `buyerType`, `siteName`, `location`, `address`, `siteLocation`, `siteClass`, `targetSiteType`, `workflow`, `category`, `targetRobotTeam`, and `message`. A request URL, location draft, or `/api/inbound-request` submission records intake only; it does not grant entitlement, package access, rights clearance, payment, provider execution, fulfillment, or hosted-session availability.

Agent access truth labels include `capture_grounded`, `provider_derived`, `generated`, `sample_demo`, `public_demo_eligible`, `request_gated`, `protected_robot_team`, and `dry_run_order`.

Current MCP tool names include `blueprint.siteWorld.search`, `blueprint.catalog.search`, `blueprint.request.locationDraft`, `blueprint.siteWorld.get`, `blueprint.siteWorld.launchReadiness`, `blueprint.commerce.quote`, `blueprint.commerce.checkoutDryRun`, `blueprint.commerce.order.get`, `blueprint.commerce.entitlement.get`, `blueprint.commerce.entitlementReadiness`, `blueprint.session.create`, `blueprint.session.reset`, `blueprint.session.step`, `blueprint.session.runBatch`, `blueprint.session.control`, `blueprint.session.renderExplorer`, and `blueprint.session.export`.

No-credential mock proof: `npm run smoke:agent-headless` exercises public discovery, dry-run quote/order/entitlement readiness, truth labels, and a mock public-demo hosted session without proving live payment, rights clearance, provider execution, package access, hosted fulfillment, or deployment readiness.

## Full Machine-Readable Overview

- [llms-full.txt](https://tryblueprint.io/llms-full.txt): longer public overview, page map, query themes, and answer guidance.
- [Public site-content API](https://tryblueprint.io/api/site-content): non-sensitive JSON summary of the same public pages.
- [Robot-team OpenAPI contract](https://tryblueprint.io/agent-access.openapi.json): machine-readable robot-team agent contract for dry-run discovery, request drafting, commerce proof, and hosted-session lifecycle tools.
